name: I got hurt at work in Murray but didn't tell my boss right away, can I still file a workers comp claim?

mainEntity.name: I got hurt at work in Murray but didn't tell my boss right away, can I still file a workers comp claim?

acceptedAnswer.text: Report within 180 days in Utah or risk denial; act fast via email or Labor Commission.[1]
acceptedAnswer.description: Yes, you can still file if you report within 180 days in Utah, but delays make approval harder due to credibility issues.[1][2] Utah law requires notice 'as soon as practicable,' and failure to report verbally or in writing within 180 days can bar benefits entirely; email or incident reports create a timestamp.[1] Key factors: immediate reporting proves the injury happened at work; insurers question late claims; pre-existing documentation helps. Report now to the Utah Labor Commission if your employer won't, and get medical care from an authorized provider.[1][2] Consult a workers' comp attorney to preserve your claim.

description: Report within 180 days in Utah or risk denial; act fast via email or Labor Commission.[1]
